summary: 隔夜重点关注：市场对潜在伊朗停火的乐观情绪推动股票上涨，而印度卢比在央行措施后延续强劲反弹。

news_list.title: Barito Renewables Hits Two-Year Low on Shareholding Concerns
news_list.summary: Shares of PT Barito Renewables Energy tumbled Monday to their lowest in more than two years after the Indonesian stock exchange flagged the company for having a highly concentrated shareholder base.

news_list.title: Japan’s Takaichi Seeks Talks With Iran’s Leader Before Ultimatum
news_list.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said she is looking to hold talks with Iran’s leader and possibly also a separate call with Donald Trump, as the clock ticks on the US president’s latest threat to bomb key Iranian infrastructure.

news_list.title: Iraq Tells Buyers to Collect Crude as Its Oil Can Cross Hormuz
news_list.summary: Iraq has told Asian traders and refiners they can load its crude as vessels carrying the country’s oil are now able to transit the Strait of Hormuz thanks to an Iranian exemption, testing buyers’ confidence in the security guarantee.

news_list.title: Ships With Qatar LNG Attempt First Hormuz Exit Since War Started
news_list.summary: Two tankers carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appear to be heading toward the Strait of Hormuz, and an exit from the Persian Gulf would mark the first export to buyers outside of the region since the war started.

news_list.title: Rupee Extends Rally After Biggest Surge in 12 Years on RBI Moves
news_list.summary: The Indian rupee climbed, extending its biggest rally in 12 years after the central bank doubled down on curbing speculation against the local currency.

news_list.title: Hedge Funds Turn Bullish on Wheat for First Time in Four Years
news_list.summary: Hedge funds have turned net bullish on wheat for the first time in nearly four years, betting on higher prices driven by dry weather in the US and a shortage of fertilizer and fuel arising from the war in the Middle East.

news_list.title: If Only the Profanity Meant an End Was in Sight
news_list.summary: Markets have a lot to weigh up, but optimism took a bruising over Easter weekend.

news_list.title: The Iran War May Be Permanently Rewiring Global Energy Security
news_list.summary: Cambridge Professor Helen Thompson warns that energy disruption triggered by the US-Israel attack on Iran is exposing deep vulnerabilities.

news_list.title: Qatar’s LNG Tankers Idle Across Asia as Export Plant Stays Shut
news_list.summary: More than four-dozen empty Qatari liquefied natural gas tankers are idling across Asia, as the nation’s export plant remains shut and the Strait of Hormuz largely closed because of the war in the Middle East.

news_list.title: Travelers Flying Out of Singapore Limited to Two Power Banks
news_list.summary: Singapore will cap the number of power banks to two for each traveler flying out of the city state to reduce the fire risk associated with lithium batteries.

news_list.title: Rising Rupee Hedging Costs May Further Dim India’s Appeal to Global Funds
news_list.summary: Foreign investors earn less after protecting against currency swings.

news_list.title: Chinese Battery-Storage Supplier Sees Shipments Doubling in 2026
news_list.summary: Beijing HyperStrong Technology Co., one of China’s top suppliers of energy-storage systems, expects to more than double shipments this year on robust demand at home and abroad.

news_list.title: Ye Just Made $33 Million in Two Nights. Will His Comeback Last?
news_list.summary: The music industry is tentatively embracing the artist formerly known as Kanye West

news_list.title: Gold Pares Losses After Report on Talks About Iran War Ceasefire
news_list.summary: Gold clawed back some losses after a report about a diplomatic push for a ceasefire to the war in Iran, even as President Donald Trump ramped up his threats of escalation to the conflict that’s roiled global markets.

news_list.title: Oil Steadies as Traders Gauge Report of Push for Iran Ceasefire
news_list.summary: Oil steadied as traders tracked a report of a push for a ceasefire in the Middle East, after President Donald Trump had announced a fresh ultimatum on Iran if the Strait of Hormuz was not reopened.

news_list.title: Stocks Rise on Optimism at Possible Iran Ceasefire: Markets Wrap
news_list.summary: Stocks rose and crude oil pared gains as investors took some comfort on signs the impact of the six-week-old Middle East conflict may be contained, and after a report that Iran is pushing for a ceasefire.
